steps.title: Compare averages and outliers
steps.content: Arthropod studies had larger differences on average than bird studies did, but every animal class had individual studies well above the arthropod average. That means some bird results exceeded at least some arthropod results.

steps.title: Keep the inference narrow
steps.content: Choice A contradicts the average pattern, Choice B makes an unsupported claim about one shore crab study, and Choice C introduces an unmentioned reporting issue. The remaining choice captures the overlap between individual studies. The answer is D.
